title: Find the K-th Largest Value in an Array
description:
Given an unsorted integer array nums and an integer k (1-indexed), return the k-th largest element in the array. Solve it in O(n log k) using a size-k min-heap so the heap always stores the current k largest elements. This reinforces priority queue maintenance and the idea that the root of a min-heap tracks the smallest among the kept candidates.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^9
- 1 ≤ k ≤ len(nums)
explanation:
Intuition
Keep only the top k elements seen so far. The smallest among them sits at the heap root, which is exactly the k-th largest overall after processing all numbers.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Create an empty min-heap
2. Main Algorithm: Push numbers; if heap grows beyond k, pop the smallest
3. Return Result: Return the heap root as the k-th largest

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n log k)
- Space Complexity: O(k)

Key Insights
- A min-heap of size k keeps memory bounded
- Root is the k-th largest after full scan

Common Pitfalls
- Using a max-heap complicates code in Python
- Forgetting to pop when size exceeds k yields wrong root
hints: Heap root equals the answer at the end
